title: Audit PPO/eval/DDP correctness and add regression tests

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Starting correctness audit of PPO, eval, DDP, seeding, and train/eval consistency before further experiments.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Implemented correctness fixes for SAME_STEP vector env handling, truncation-aware bootstrap, deterministic main-rank eval, weighted episode metrics, checkpoint resume, and added regression tests. pytest is green.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Completed PPO/eval/DDP audit and fixes. Added SAME_STEP env handling, truncation bootstrap from final_obs, deterministic rank-0 eval with broadcast, checkpoint resume with RNG state, weighted episode metrics, and regression tests. pytest is green.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Completed PPO/env/eval audit and fixes. Corrected SAME_STEP autoreset handling, truncation bootstrap from final_obs, non-overlapping reset seeds, rank-0 deterministic evaluation/broadcast, weighted episode metric reduction, checkpoint resume/RNG restore, and config override persistence. Added regression tests. Verification: pytest -q passes (DDP smoke skips when loopback sockets are unavailable in this environment).

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Combined DoorKey report published at outputs/reports/lss_combined_doorkey_report.md. Combined six-seed external-greedy means: recovered token_dense 0.4505, KL learner-state token_dense 0.6042, baseline PPO SARE 0.0000, KL learner-state SARE 0.8568 with no complete-seed failures.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Published the combined DoorKey claim report at outputs/reports/lss_combined_doorkey_report.md (+ .csv). The report consolidates original matched controls and the fresh 23/29/31 matched-control lane into one six-seed external-eval view and explicitly concludes that the DoorKey routed edge is stronger under teacher-guided KL learner-state supervision.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Implemented SARE asymmetric routed experts with route entropy, path coverage, and expert load metrics. Verified one-update smoke run.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Implemented TREG-H with second-hop routing, halting head, transition bias, and ponder penalty. Verified one-update smoke run.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Implemented SRW with unary routed experts, salient-token selection, and gated relational attention. Verified one-update smoke run.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Implemented POR with persistent differentiable option state, termination, duration tracking, and switch-rate metrics. Verified one-update smoke run.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Ran the exact same KL learner-state method on KeyCorridor for seeds 7/11/19 under outputs/experiments/lss_claim_hardening/keycorridor. External 64-episode greedy success stayed 0.0 for recovered token_dense, baseline PPO SARE, and KL learner-state SARE on all three seeds. Report: outputs/reports/lss_keycorridor_transfer_report.md.

title: Reproduce current learner-state multiseed baseline
close_reason: Reproduced the current learner-state multiseed baseline in outputs/reproductions/lss_robustness_baseline and published outputs/reports/lss_robustness_reproduction_note.md. Results align with the accepted teacher-extraction lane closely enough to support the robustness sweep.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Validated the new learner-state robustness scripts and analysis entrypoint locally. Starting a fresh 3-seed DoorKey reproduction under the external 64-episode evaluation path from outputs/reproductions/lss_robustness_baseline.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Fresh 3-seed baseline reproduction completed in outputs/reproductions/lss_robustness_baseline. External 64-episode eval matches the accepted teacher-extraction lane closely: seed 7 LSS SARE 0.5, seed 11 LSS SARE 1.0, seed 19 LSS SARE 0.0; recovered token_dense remains seed-sensitive at 0.703/0.0/1.0. See outputs/reports/lss_robustness_reproduction_note.md.

title: Write final greedy-recovery decision memo
close_reason: done: final greedy-recovery decision memo published with evidence-backed stop recommendation

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Published the final no-go narrative in report.md, summary.md, and outputs/reports/greedy_recovery_decision_memo.md, linking the reproduction, checkpoint dynamics, entropy schedule, and self-imitation artifacts. Recommendation: stop routed work for greedy DoorKey claims in this repo unless a new extraction method is justified as a separate project.

title: Run entropy schedule sweep for single_expert and sare
close_reason: done: bounded entropy schedules do not recover greedy DoorKey for single_expert or sare

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Ran source .venv/bin/activate && ./scripts/run_entropy_schedule_sweep.sh, then regenerated outputs/reports/entropy_schedule_report.md and .csv after fixing the launcher to point at the completed output root. Evidence: no tested schedule recovered nonzero greedy DoorKey for single_expert or sare. Best sampled schedules were late_linear:0.01->0.001@0.75 for both variants, but greedy success remained 0.0.

title: Teacher-guided extraction for routed DoorKey policies

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Completed the teacher-guided extraction phase. Offline distillation was negative, learner-state supervision partially recovered greedy SARE, route integrity held on the best seed, and the 3-seed check was not robust enough to reopen routed greedy claims. Evidence: outputs/reports/teacher_extraction_reproduction_note.md, outputs/reports/policy_distillation_report.md, outputs/reports/learner_state_supervision_report.md, outputs/reports/teacher_extraction_multiseed_report.md, outputs/reports/teacher_extraction_decision_memo.md.

title: Consolidate the DoorKey teacher-guided SARE claim

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Starting claim-consolidation phase: reproduce current DoorKey baseline, run fresh matched teacher-guided token_dense controls on the added-seed lane, and run causal route-dependence probes on recovered SARE checkpoints. Final claims will use the external 64-episode evaluation path only.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Claim-consolidation phase completed. Fresh matched DoorKey teacher-guided controls still favor SARE (fresh mean 1.0000 vs 0.5417 for KL token_dense; combined six-seed means 0.8568 vs 0.6042) and causal route-dependence probes on recovered seeds 7 and 23 drop greedy success from 1.0 to 0.0 under every bounded routing perturbation. Final memo: outputs/reports/lss_claim_consolidation_decision_memo.md.

title: Implement offline policy distillation path
description: Add minimal teacher-policy dataset harvest and supervised student fine-tune for DoorKey, reusing the existing analysis/reporting conventions where possible.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Implemented the initial offline teacher-distillation runner, YAML spec loader, sweep script, and smoke test. Focused CPU smoke tests for policy_distillation and learner_state_supervision passed.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Implemented offline policy distillation path in src/psmn_rl/analysis/policy_distillation.py with sweep script and configs under configs/experiments/teacher_extraction/. Evidence: outputs/reports/policy_distillation_report.md.

title: Add checkpoint dynamics analysis
close_reason: done: checkpoint dynamics rule out missed transient greedy checkpoints as the main explanation

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Added optional checkpoint archiving (system.archive_checkpoints), checkpoint-series analysis in src/psmn_rl/analysis/checkpoint_dynamics.py, DoorKey checkpoint-scan configs for single_expert and sare, and smoke coverage in tests/test_checkpoint_dynamics_smoke.py. Ran: source .venv/bin/activate && ./scripts/run_checkpoint_dynamics.sh, then regenerated outputs/reports/checkpoint_dynamics_report.md and .csv. Evidence: neither single_expert nor sare ever showed a nonzero greedy checkpoint anywhere in the archived DoorKey series, while sampled success rose to 0.75 and 1.0 respectively.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Epic completed. Final repo conclusion: flat_dense remains the strongest greedy DoorKey control; the smallest successful tokenized recovery is token_dense with ent_coef=0.001; matched SARE still loses under greedy DoorKey evaluation. See report.md, summary.md, outputs/reports/policy_extraction_report.md, outputs/reports/tokenization_gap_report.md, and outputs/experiments/sare_retest/report.md.

title: MiniGrid baseline recovery and routing re-evaluation

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Recovery epic completed. The smoke/falsification lane remains in configs/baseline/ and the original ablation scripts; the canonical recovery lane now lives in configs/diagnostic/ and configs/experiments/. Verification included pytest -q in the repo uv environment plus all-GPU smoke verification of scripts/run_tiny_overfit.sh, scripts/run_minigrid_sanity_suite.sh, scripts/run_minigrid_baseline_suite.sh, and scripts/run_sare_comparison_sweep.sh with PSMN_MAX_UPDATES=1. Durable local reports were generated under outputs/reports/.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Published the final claim-hardening decision memo at outputs/reports/lss_claim_hardening_decision_memo.md. Conclusion: the DoorKey teacher-guided SARE claim is stronger on fresh seeds and still interesting after matched tokenized controls, but it remains narrowly scoped because the bounded KeyCorridor transfer check is flat.

title: Baseline solvability and PPO audit campaign

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Starting the control-first audit/fix campaign: PPO correctness first, then sanity-tier overfit and stronger controls.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Completed the control-first PPO audit campaign. The harness is now trustworthy enough for baseline solvability work: Empty solves for flat/token dense, DoorKey solves for flat_dense, and matched SARE still loses on DoorKey.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Completed the control-first PPO audit campaign. Easy-task overfit and sanity tiers now work, fully observed DoorKey is solved by a tuned token_dense control, and partial DoorKey/Memory expose a greedy-vs-sampled evaluation mismatch that must be resolved before fair routed comparisons.

title: Write learner-state robustness decision memo
close_reason: Final learner-state robustness decision memo published.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Published outputs/reports/lss_robustness_decision_memo.md. Final answer: teacher-logit KL learner-state supervision is robust enough to reopen the routed DoorKey claim under a teacher-guided extraction framing, but not as a PPO-alone routed win.

title: Add MiniGrid sanity tier and tiny overfit protocol

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Starting sanity-tier and overfit experiments with flat_dense and token_dense using the new configs and scripts.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Added sanity-tier configs and scripts, plus tiny overfit protocol. Empty-5x5 now solves for flat_dense and token_dense in the sanity sweep; overfit report saved at overfit_report.md and outputs/experiments/minigrid_overfit/.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Added configs/minigrid/sanity/, scripts/run_minigrid_overfit.sh, scripts/run_minigrid_sanity_sweep.sh, and overfit_report.md. Verified overfit on MiniGrid-Empty-5x5-v0: flat_dense and token_dense both reached greedy eval_success_rate 1.0; sanity sweep also solved Empty-5x5 for both controls.

title: Harden the reopened teacher-guided SARE claim

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Claim-hardening phase complete. DoorKey teacher-guided KL learner-state SARE reproduced cleanly, strengthened on fresh seeds 23/29/31, and remained ahead of the matched teacher-guided token_dense mean on the original 7/11/19 lane. The newly recovered DoorKey seed 23 still shows meaningful routing, but the exact same method is flat on KeyCorridor. Canonical artifacts: outputs/reports/lss_claim_hardening_reproduction_note.md, outputs/reports/lss_additional_seed_report.md, outputs/reports/lss_matched_control_report.md, outputs/reports/lss_new_case_route_integrity_report.md, outputs/reports/lss_keycorridor_transfer_report.md, outputs/reports/lss_claim_hardening_decision_memo.md.

title: Reproduce accepted DoorKey flat_dense, token_dense, and SARE runs

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Starting exact reproduction of the accepted DoorKey flat_dense, token_dense, and SARE runs in the experiment lane. The baseline test gate passed via source .venv/bin/activate && pytest -q before the reruns.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Current-head DoorKey reproductions match the accepted eval-side conclusion (flat_dense solves; token_dense and SARE remain at zero greedy eval), but train-side metrics drift from the published artifacts. Accepted artifacts record git_commit=59027bb while the reproduction was run on current main; debugging provenance/reproducibility before policy diagnostics.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Saved outputs/reports/reproduction_note.md with exact current-head DoorKey reproduction commands, configs, checkpoints, and metrics. Result: flat_dense still solves greedy DoorKey; token_dense and matched SARE still fail greedy DoorKey. Exact train-side curve reproduction is not possible from the old artifact metadata because run_meta only recorded git_commit and the accepted artifacts point at 59027bb, which cannot uniquely reconstruct the code state. Added git_dirty recording for future runs and verified it with pytest.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Reproduction work completed earlier in this phase. outputs/reports/reproduction_note.md records the exact DoorKey commands, configs, checkpoints, and metrics. The accepted evaluation ranking reproduces on current main, while old train-curve provenance remained incomplete because older run_meta only recorded git_commit without git_dirty.

title: Fix report links and standardize external-eval decision path
close_reason: Doc sync complete. Top-level narrative now points to the current learner-state robustness phase and the external 64-episode gate via report.md and summary.md.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Updated report.md and summary.md to point at the robust learner-state phase and the external 64-episode decision path. New top-level narrative now references outputs/reports/lss_robustness_decision_memo.md and the new route-integrity / sweep / multiseed artifacts.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Reproduction artifact generated with: source .venv/bin/activate && ./scripts/run_lss_claim_broadening_reproduction.sh. Evidence: outputs/reports/lss_claim_broadening_reproduction_note.md and outputs/reports/lss_claim_broadening_reproduction_note.csv.

title: Run tokenization-gap diagnostics and minimal ablations

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Policy-extraction diagnostics are done enough to isolate the next question. DoorKey result on current experiment lane: flat_dense is strong and deterministic under greedy; token_dense remains weak even under sampled eval (best 0.125 success at T=0.5); single_expert and SARE both jump from 0.0 greedy success to 1.0 sampled success at T=1.0. Next step: tokenization-gap diagnostics around fully observed DoorKey, token mixing depth, and representation collapse metrics to find the smallest control-side intervention that materially improves token_dense.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Completed tokenization-gap diagnostics. Ran baseline, fully observed, depth-4, and depth-4+fully-observed token_dense DoorKey probes under outputs/diagnostics/tokenization_gap and summarized them in outputs/reports/tokenization_gap_report.md. Main result: full observation helps sampled competence, depth alone does not fix partial-observation token_dense, and greedy extraction remains a separate bottleneck.

title: Conditional multiseed gate for improved learner-state SARE
close_reason: 3-seed external gate complete and positive. Improved learner-state SARE beats recovered token_dense mean success and eliminates complete-seed failure.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Promoting the plain KL learner-state method to the 3-seed external gate. Tokenized-student sanity will use the same KL append_all variant on the minimal single-seed check required by the memo.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Conditional multiseed gate completed in outputs/reports/lss_robustness_multiseed_report.md. Improved learner-state SARE reaches greedy 1.0 / 0.5625 / 0.5781 on seeds 7 / 11 / 19, mean 0.7135, beating recovered token_dense mean 0.5677 with no zero-seed failures.

title: Reproduce current DoorKey KL learner-state baseline

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Reproduction note generated from the published external-eval artifact lanes to avoid redundant re-evaluation of already-accepted DoorKey roots. Artifact: outputs/reports/lss_claim_consolidation_reproduction_note.md. Sources: outputs/reports/lss_claim_hardening_reproduction_note.csv and outputs/reports/lss_additional_seed_report.csv plus current root metadata.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Reproduced the current DoorKey baseline on the external 64-episode path with: source .venv/bin/activate && ./scripts/run_lss_claim_consolidation_reproduction.sh. Artifact: outputs/reports/lss_claim_consolidation_reproduction_note.md (+ .csv). Evidence confirms the original and fresh SARE lanes remain close to the published baselines before fresh matched controls or route probes.

title: Conditional route-integrity report for new recovered case

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Added a route-integrity comparison for a newly recovered DoorKey seed at outputs/reports/lss_new_case_route_integrity_report.md. On seed 23, KL learner-state SARE moved greedy success from 0.0 to 1.0 while route entropy stayed near the PPO baseline (1.3857 -> 1.3804) and active compute stayed fixed at 0.5.

title: Run staged learner-state robustness sweep
close_reason: Bounded learner-state robustness sweep complete. KL append_all is the only improving method; capped replay variants are negative. See outputs/reports/lss_robustness_sweep_report.md.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Starting staged learner-state robustness sweep. Stage A will compare hard-label baseline vs teacher-logit KL supervision on the diagnostic seeds 7 and 19 under the same external 64-episode evaluation path.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Stage A/B result: target type matters, aggregation does not. KL learner-state supervision lifts seed 7 to 1.0 greedy and seed 19 to 0.578 under the external 64-episode path, while both cap_recent and cap_recent_balanced collapse back to 0.0 on seed 7 and fail to lift seed 19.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Stage A/B robustness sweep completed and published in outputs/reports/lss_robustness_sweep_report.md. KL teacher-logit targets are the only improving axis; cap_recent and cap_recent_balanced are both negative.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Launching bounded DoorKey entropy sweep on tokenized controls only. Runs: token_dense ent_coef in {1e-3, 0.0} and single_expert ent_coef in {1e-3, 0.0}. This is directly justified by the policy-extraction result: single_expert already solves under sampled eval but fails under greedy, while token_dense remains soft and underconfident. Goal: recover at least one greedy-competent tokenized control before any routed retest.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Completed bounded tokenized-control recovery sweep. The smallest successful intervention was lowering token_dense PPO ent_coef from 0.01 to 0.001, which recovered greedy DoorKey success to 0.75 and improved representation health. token_dense ent_coef=0.0 and both single_expert entropy variants did not recover a greedy-competent control.

title: Inspect DoorKey tokenized-control bottlenecks in current code
close_reason: Inspection complete; ranked likely tokenized-control bottlenecks in current DoorKey code with exact model/train/eval/config references and minimal follow-up interventions.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Inspecting current DoorKey control code paths to rank likely bottlenecks in tokenized vs flat_dense performance, with focus on obs encoding, pooling, mixing depth, actor/value inputs, and greedy eval behavior.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Findings: strongest shared bottlenecks appear to be token-level mean pooling of all MiniGrid cells plus a lightly weighted direction token, actor/value heads consuming only pooled features, and no cross-token mixing at all in single_expert/SARE beyond pooling. Train always samples while eval defaults to greedy argmax, so sampled eval should be checked before concluding policy learning is absent.

title: Conditional multi-seed validation
description: Only if teacher-guided extraction yields a real positive SARE signal, run a small multi-seed validation against recovered token_dense.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Ran 3-seed validation with ./scripts/run_teacher_extraction_multiseed.sh plus seed-specific 64-episode policy_diagnostics. Learner-state SARE reached greedy success 0.5, 1.0, and 0.0 across seeds 7, 11, and 19 respectively, so the gain is real but not robust. Evidence: outputs/reports/teacher_extraction_multiseed_report.md.

title: Robust learner-state supervision for SARE
close_reason: Robust learner-state supervision for SARE complete and verified. Final external gate passes for KL learner-state supervision on DoorKey, with route integrity preserved on the revived seed.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: All learner-state robustness workstreams are complete. Verification: pytest -q passes; bash -n on new scripts passes; outputs/reports now contains reproduction, heterogeneity, sweep, multiseed, route-integrity, and decision-memo artifacts.

title: Run sanity and hard-task control sweeps, update reports

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Completed sanity dense sweep, hard-task dense sweep, matched DoorKey SARE rerun, and updated root-level report.md/summary.md/overfit_report.md with conclusions.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Ran the control-first experiment set and wrote root-level summaries. Key evidence: overfit_report.md, outputs/minigrid/overfit/summary.md, outputs/minigrid/sanity_sweep/summary.md, outputs/minigrid/diagnostic_runs/doorkey_fullobs_token_dense_tuned/summary.md, outputs/minigrid/main_runs/doorkey_token_dense_tuned/summary.md, outputs/minigrid/diagnostic_runs/eval_mode_checks.md.

title: Conditional route-integrity report for improved learner-state SARE
close_reason: Route integrity check complete on the revived seed 19. Routing remains meaningfully used; no obvious route collapse.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Published outputs/reports/lss_route_integrity_report.md for the revived seed 19. Improved KL learner-state SARE lifts greedy success to 0.5938 while keeping route entropy and active compute essentially unchanged and expert loads balanced.

title: Implement learner-state supervision loop
description: Add bounded learner-state teacher querying and aggregation for DoorKey to test whether covariate shift blocks SARE distillation.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Implemented and ran learner-state supervision loop in src/psmn_rl/analysis/learner_state_supervision.py. flat_dense->SARE improved greedy success from 0.0 to 0.5 on seed 7 and to 1.0 on seed 11; flat_dense->token_dense remained at 0.0 in the tested lane. Evidence: outputs/reports/learner_state_supervision_report.md and outputs/reports/teacher_extraction_sare_compare_64.md.

title: Extend causal route-dependence probes to more recovered SARE seeds

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Ran source .venv/bin/activate && ./scripts/run_lss_claim_broadening_extended_route_dependence.sh. Evidence: outputs/reports/lss_extended_route_dependence_report.md and outputs/reports/lss_extended_route_dependence_report.csv.

title: Run causal route-dependence probes on recovered SARE

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Causal route-dependence probes completed on recovered SARE seeds original=7 and fresh=23. Every single-expert ablation, fixed-router override, and random routing perturbation dropped greedy DoorKey success from 1.0 to 0.0 while active compute stayed at 0.5; report: outputs/reports/lss_causal_route_dependence_report.md.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Ran bounded causal route-dependence probes with scripts/run_lss_claim_consolidation_route_dependence.sh. Artifact: outputs/reports/lss_causal_route_dependence_report.md (+ .csv). On recovered seeds 7 and 23, every single-expert ablation, fixed-router override, and route-randomization probe drops greedy success from 1.0000 to 0.0000, which materially supports causal routing dependence.

title: Update experiment scripts and reporting for control-first comparisons

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Completed new control-first scripts and reporting docs: run_minigrid_overfit.sh, run_minigrid_sanity_dense.sh, run_minigrid_hard_dense.sh, run_minigrid_sare_compare.sh, README.md, AGENTS.md, summary.md, report.md, overfit_report.md.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Added scripts/run_minigrid_control_sweep.sh, scripts/run_sare_comparison_sweep.sh, scripts/eval_policy_modes.sh, and updated README/AGENTS/report.md/summary.md/overfit_report.md for the control-first harness.

title: Reproduce current DoorKey greedy-recovery baseline
close_reason: done: current DoorKey greedy-recovery baseline reproduced cleanly on current main

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Reproduced the current DoorKey calibration baseline with: source .venv/bin/activate && PSMN_OUTPUT_ROOT=outputs/reproductions/greedy_recovery_baseline PSMN_EVAL_EPISODES=32 ./scripts/run_sare_fair_retest.sh. Output artifacts: outputs/reproductions/greedy_recovery_baseline/report.md and outputs/reports/greedy_recovery_reproduction_note.md. Reproduced ranking matches published calibration artifacts: flat_dense greedy success 1.0, token_dense_ent1e3 greedy success 0.75, single_expert_ent1e3 greedy success 0.0 with sampled success 0.75, sare_ent1e3 greedy success 0.0 with sampled success 1.0.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Fresh DoorKey DDP reruns are currently blocked by a runtime issue outside the trainer: nvidia-smi still lists 4 GPUs, but fresh PyTorch CUDA init now fails with cudaGetDeviceCount error 304 and torchrun standalone falls into a bad localhost:0 rendezvous path before model code starts. Existing accepted artifact roots and previously reproduced outputs remain readable. Captured evidence with: nvidia-smi -L; python -c 'import torch; print(torch.cuda.is_available(), torch.cuda.device_count())'; repeated torchrun retries via scripts/run_sare_fair_retest.sh.

title: Add seed-heterogeneity diagnostics for learner-state supervision
close_reason: Seed-heterogeneity diagnostics added and published in outputs/reports/lss_seed_heterogeneity_report.md. The report identifies stale append-all aggregation as the more plausible brittleness source than low teacher confidence.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Instrumented learner-state collection with dataset-size, unique-state, teacher-confidence, disagreement, and route-metric diagnostics. The next step is to regenerate the 3-seed baseline so the heterogeneity report is based on fresh round-level data.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Published outputs/reports/lss_seed_heterogeneity_report.md from the fresh 3-seed baseline. Main finding: teacher confidence is high on all seeds by later rounds; the more plausible failure mode is learner-state optimization around the append-all dataset rather than weak teacher labels.

title: Audit PPO correctness and baseline solvability harness
Add policy-extraction diagnostics for DoorKey controls

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Implementing action-selection diagnostics for DoorKey: rollout action entropy/max-prob/logit-margin/greedy-match logging, temperature-scaled eval, and a compact policy_diagnostics analysis script. Next run: flat_dense/token_dense/single_expert/SARE on DoorKey with multi-mode eval to test whether the tokenized gap is mainly policy extraction.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Completed DoorKey policy-extraction diagnostics. Added rollout/eval action entropy, max-prob, logit-margin, greedy-match logging; added temperature-scaled eval; ran outputs/diagnostics/policy_extraction/doorkey and saved outputs/reports/policy_extraction_report.md. Main result: flat_dense is sharp under greedy; token_dense is weak even under sampled eval; single_expert and SARE are strong under sampled eval but fail greedy.

title: Minimal tokenized-student sanity check for best learner-state variant
close_reason: Minimal tokenized-student sanity check complete and folded into outputs/reports/lss_robustness_sweep_report.md.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Minimal tokenized-student sanity check completed with the winning KL append_all method. On seed 19, flat_dense -> token_dense stays positive at greedy 1.0, so the improved learner-state method is not a SARE-only artifact.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: No separate failure-forensics pass was needed. The additional-seed replication was uniformly positive on DoorKey and the matched-control comparison was decisive enough to move directly to the bounded KeyCorridor transfer check.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Optional teacher-dependence mini-check was not needed in this phase. Fresh matched teacher-guided token_dense controls plus causal route-dependence probes resolved the main ambiguity without opening another method axis.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Teacher-dependence mini-check was not needed in this phase. Fresh matched teacher-guided token_dense controls did not erase the routed DoorKey edge, and the causal route-dependence probes were already decisive enough to answer the consolidation question. Leaving the teacher-dependence branch unused keeps the phase bounded as requested.

title: Doc sync for tokenized-control recovery phase

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Starting with doc sync against the accepted outputs/reports artifacts so the repo narrative matches the published evidence before reproducing DoorKey controls.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Updated top-level narrative docs to match the accepted outputs/reports artifacts. report.md and summary.md now explicitly state that flat_dense is the best verified DoorKey control, accepted SARE still loses, and the next phase is tokenized-control recovery rather than more routing variants.

title: Implement self-imitation / successful-trajectory distillation
close_reason: done: self-imitation from successful sampled trajectories does not recover greedy single_expert or greedy sare

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Reused the existing self-imitation lane, tightened it to use reproduced baseline checkpoints and deterministic harvest/fine-tune seeding, and ran CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1,2,3 ./scripts/run_self_imitation_sweep.sh. Artifacts: outputs/reports/self_imitation_report.md and outputs/reports/self_imitation_report.csv. Evidence: all 8 self-imitation runs kept greedy success at 0.0 for both single_expert and sare.

title: Conditional route-integrity check
description: Only if teacher-guided extraction materially improves SARE, report whether routing metrics collapse or remain meaningfully routed under the improved policy.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Route-integrity checks on recovered SARE show meaningful routing remains. Best recovered seed kept active_compute_proxy at 0.5 and only modestly reduced route entropy while reaching greedy success 1.0. Evidence: outputs/reports/distilled_route_integrity_best_seed_report.md.

title: Strengthen dense controls and add recurrent memory baseline

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Sanity tier: flat_dense and token_dense both solve Empty-5x5. Hard controls: flat_dense solves DoorKey at the new baseline budget, tokenized controls do not yet solve DoorKey or KeyCorridor, and Memory still needs more work before fair routed comparison.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Added token_gru recurrent control, new baseline config tiers, and ran hard-task control sweep. flat_dense solves DoorKey under the corrected harness; tokenized controls remain weak on DoorKey/KeyCorridor and Memory still needs more work.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Added token_gru recurrent control, checkpoint resume support, main-task control configs, and control runs on Memory. At 60 updates, token_gru showed nontrivial learning; sampled eval reached return 0.474 and success 0.5. A matched token_dense Memory control was also run for comparison.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Auditing the existing PPO/eval/DDP fixes, adding the requested tests where coverage is still thin, and reconciling the recovery configs into dedicated diagnostic/experiments lanes.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Completed correctness audit. Added explicit tests for GAE/returns and truncated reward bootstrapping in tests/test_rollout_storage.py, checkpoint-based greedy eval reproducibility in tests/test_eval_consistency.py, and DDP eval path smoke coverage in tests/test_ddp_eval_smoke.py. Full gate passes in the repo venv via pytest -q.
